cards.name: Weeping Blade
cards.description: Deal [damage:2] damage twice. If the target is Bleeding, attack a third time.

meta.whyDifferent: Cleavers buys its extra attack with Frenzy (you got hit); this buys it with the foe Bleeding - a hit-COUNT payoff for bleed. Bleeding ticks whenever the holder is dealt damage (Bleeding.json: effectTrigger DealtDamage), so three small hits = three bleed ticks. New payoff mode: bleed decks currently cash out stacks ([[other(status)Bleeding]] readers), not tick frequency.
meta.intendedDeck: STR bleed-tempo. Turn 1: Lacerate/Open Old Wounds to start the bleed, then Weeping Blade converts every attack into double value. Pairs with Spiteful Ground (this pack) for tick saturation.
meta.powerNotes: 1-energy common baseline ~4 dmg (spec 4). Base 2+2=4; conditional third hit = 6 total plus 3 bleed ticks, paid for by the setup card (spec 4: conditional may exceed baseline by setup value). Precedent: Cleavers (Cleavers.json, C, 1 Neutral) = 2 + conditional (2 dmg + 3 Bleeding). Condition idiom StatusOnOpponent Is 'bleeding' verified in Wildclaw.json / Venomous_Bite.json (decoded conds [62:bleeding]).

meta.whyDifferent: Withering Touch is 0-cost poison gated on Synergy; this is 0-cost bleed whose poison rider is gated on Frenzy (took damage this turn - life costs count, GAME-MECHANICS Part I). New decision: the attrition deck wants to get hit / pay blood BEFORE playing it, linking this cluster to the CrimsonLedger blood cluster without requiring it.
meta.intendedDeck: Any DEX/STR attrition shell; best in blood-cost hybrids where Frenzy is free. Feeds Bloodlust checks (Gutter Toxin) and Weeping Blade in the same pack.
meta.powerNotes: 0-cost common baseline ~2-3 dmg-equivalent (spec 4). 2 Bleeding ~ 2 damage over 2 ticks; conditional 2 Poison ~ 2 more. Precedent: Withering_Touch.json (0-cost C, 3 Poison + conditional 3). Frenzy condition verified in Cleavers.json (decoded conds [422:]).

meta.whyDifferent: Toxicity (DEX 1 C) is 5 Poison + 1 Potency unconditionally. This trades raw stacks for a Bloodlust gate (dealt bleeding damage this turn or target is Bleeding, ConditionChecker): the Potency doubles only if you sequenced bleed FIRST. No shipped poison card reads a bleed condition; the existing bridge (Festering_Wounds.json) goes the other direction (bleed-inflicts add Poison). New decision: play order across the two archetypes.
meta.intendedDeck: DEX/STR dual-attrition. Lacerate/Open Old Wounds turn 1, Gutter Toxin turn 2 with Bloodlust live; Potency then boosts BOTH poison and bleeding ticks (Potency applies to every affliction tick - SpellEffects.StatusDamage, GAME-MECHANICS Part II).
meta.powerNotes: Vs Toxicity (Toxicity.json): -1 Poison, conditional +1 Potency net. Not strictly better/worse (power-creep gate). Bloodlust condition verified in Vampirism.json / Grievous_Injury.json (decoded conds [432:]). bless+Potency idiom verified in Contaminate.json / Witching_Hour.json.

meta.nearestExisting: Toxicity.json (shape); Buzzbite.json (only Infected-inflict idiom in pool, Monster-rarity)
meta.whyDifferent: No player-pool card inflicts Infected - all six Infected users are Monster cards (Buzzbite, Rend, Talonlash, Dark_Skittering, Hostess__Presence, Pestilent_Authority). The pairing is the point: Infected is wiped when the holder GAINS health (Infected.json clearAt GainHealth), but Poison eats incoming healing 1:1 before it lands (DoHealing) - so the Poison half literally shields the Infected half from small heals. A synergy that exists only because of verified status timing, surfaced as a card.
meta.intendedDeck: DEX affliction stacking. Core common of the Infected package (Quarantine, Ague Fever, Pyrrhic Remedy). Infected ticks its full stacks per card the foe discards (Infected.json effectTrigger Discard) - enemies discard their unplayed hand every turn (MonsterHandler turn loop).
meta.powerNotes: Vs Toxicity (DEX 1 C, 5 Poison + 1 Potency): -1 Poison, and 1 Infected ~ 1-3 dmg/turn until wiped, symmetric value. inflict+referenceStatus Infected idiom verified in Buzzbite.json.

cards.name: Rupture
cards.description: Deal [damage:3] damage. On Hit: If the target has 5 or more Bleeding, inflict a Deep Wound.

meta.whyDifferent: Every shipped Deep Wound setup keys off the ATTACK context: Flanking (Cutting_Words), crit (Headshot), even HP (Dividing_Strike), 3rd card (Vigorating_Strike), Flow (Follow_Through), basic count (Duel_Mastery). None keys off the bleed STACK the archetype already builds. This makes stacked Bleeding (which otherwise wastes stacks - it loses 1 per tick and big stacks outlive the fight) a Deep Wound battery: new payoff for an existing setup.
meta.intendedDeck: STR big-bleed. Lacerate/Boneskewer pile stacks, Rupture converts overstacked bleed into wound-execute pressure (5 Deep Wounds slay, SpellEffects.DeepWound).
meta.powerNotes: 3 dmg at 1 STR common is under the 4-dmg baseline; the gap pays for the conditional Deep Wound (Cutting_Words: 1 dmg + Flanking-gated DW at same cost/rarity). Dual-condition deepwound idiom verified in Cutting_Words.json (decoded conds [251:[[lastDamage]]:0 ; 372:]); [[other(status)Bleeding]] token verified in Vampirism.json / Heartache.json.

cards.name: Quarantine
cards.description: Inflict 2 Infected. The target discards all Heal cards.

meta.whyDifferent: Triple-purpose line no shipped card has: (1) the forced discards each trigger Infected immediately (Infected.json effectTrigger Discard - it ticks per card the holder discards), (2) discarding their Heal cards removes the only thing that wipes Infected (clearAt GainHealth), (3) it is hand-disruption in an archetype that has none. Silence discards heal/hex cards but carries no affliction and is not in the player pool (expansion None).
meta.intendedDeck: DEX Infected tempo. Best against sustain enemies; alongside Septic Bloom and Ague Fever it makes Infected stick long enough to matter.
meta.powerNotes: forcediscard:heal verified verbatim in Silence.json (its text: 'The target discards all Heal and Hex cards'); targets enemy Heal-hint (MonsterHint) cards. 2 Infected + situational discard at 1 energy U ~ Serrated_Arrow-tier utility. UNVERIFIED: whether each forcediscard'ed card fires the Discard trigger for Infected ticks (assumed; needs sim/in-game check).

meta.whyDifferent: No card in the entire pool - player or monster - inflicts Sickened (grep confirms zero users; it ships as a status asset with a live RunStatusEffect case). The pairing is causal, not cosmetic: Sickened strips 1 enemy energy per PlayPhase (Sickened.json), the enemy plays fewer cards, so MORE cards rot in their hand and get discarded at end of turn - and every one of those discards is an Infected tick. Energy denial that literally feeds the DoT.
meta.intendedDeck: DEX Infected control vs high-energy elites. With Septic Bloom/Quarantine as the package's tick base.
meta.powerNotes: Enemy max energy is 5 (MonsterHandler.GetMaxEnergy), so 2 Sickened ~ -1 enemy card for 2 turns, plus 1 Infected. Comparable to 1-cost U control (Sleeping_Poison.json: 2 delayed Jinxed at DEX 1 U). UNVERIFIED: Sickened has never shipped on a card; behavior vs enemies needs an in-game check (RunStatusEffect case exists and is side-agnostic).

cards.name: Salt the Wound
cards.description: This combat, whenever you inflict a Deep Wound, inflict 3 Poison and 2 Bleeding.

meta.whyDifferent: Cruelty draws on the Deepwound trigger; Festering_Wounds chains bleed-inflict into Poison. This is the third corner neither covers: Deep Wound events (the bleed archetype's execute mechanic) become AFFLICTION fuel, pulling the STR wound deck toward the DEX poison deck. The Deepwound->afflictions combination has no shipped user.
meta.intendedDeck: DEXSTR wound-affliction hybrid: Cutting Words / Rupture / Follow Through generate Deep Wounds; each one now also loads 3 Poison + 2 Bleeding, which Gutter Toxin's Potency amplifies.
meta.powerNotes: Festering_Wounds (U, DEXSTR 1) pays 2 Poison per bleed-inflict, a much more frequent trigger; Deep Wounds land ~1-2/turn in a dedicated deck, so 5 stacks/proc is proportional. Deepwound enchantment trigger verified in Cruelty.json (decoded ENCH trig 66); enchant:self + combat enchantment shape verified in Carnage.json / Festering_Wounds.json (theType 0, combatEnchantment 1).

cards.name: Symbiotic Carapace
cards.description: This combat, whenever Poison damage is dealt, gain 1 Armor.

meta.nearestExisting: Bolgar_Blightbane.json (Monster-only: heal 3 per enemy poison tick)
meta.whyDifferent: All player-pool poison payoffs read STACKS ([[other(status)Poison]]: Plague Strike, Venomancing, Aura of Venom, Cloudkill) or inflict-events (Avatar of Blight, Vulture Soul). None pays off the TICKS - poison fires once per card the holder plays (Poison.json effectTrigger PlayAction), so card-spammy enemies tick 3-5 times on their own turn. Each tick banks 1 Armor mid-enemy-turn, exactly when it protects, and Armor fading at StartPhase (Armor.json) makes the income self-balancing. Player-side version of a design only Bolgar (Monster) has.
meta.intendedDeck: Defensive DEX poison. Land 10+ Poison, then this converts the enemy's own tempo into your mitigation. Anti-synergy warning: Ague Fever's Sickened reduces enemy plays and thus this card's income - a real deck-building choice inside the pack.
meta.powerNotes: ~3-5 Armor per enemy turn while poison holds ~ sustained defensive rare at 2 energy (cf. Spectral_Guardian.json, DEXSTR 2 R, reduce damage by foe affliction count). InflictStatusDamage trigger + DamageType Is poison combination verified verbatim in Bolgar_Blightbane.json (ENCH trig 78, conds [702:poison]). DELIBERATE simplification: Bolgar adds a side-gating condition (decoded [342:], EnemyTurn) whose relative-vs-absolute semantics are unverified; I dropped it, so the text is honestly symmetric ('whenever Poison damage is dealt') - if the player is poisoned, their own ticks also feed it. Flagged for sim check.

cards.name: Pyrrhic Remedy
cards.description: Inflict 3 Infected. This combat, whenever your foe gains HEALTH, they gain 3 Poison.

enchantment.text: Whenever you gain HEALTH, gain 3 Poison.

meta.whyDifferent: Closes the Infected loop no shipped card addresses: Infected's escape hatch is healing (clearAt GainHealth), so this makes the escape itself toxic - the heal wipes Infected but banks 3 Poison, and Poison then eats their NEXT heal 1:1 (DoHealing), re-protecting any reapplied Infected. Bone Pin blocks healing flat for one turn; this converts healing into afflictions for the whole combat: a punish, not a lock, and a new payoff for the natural 'enemy heals' setup.
meta.intendedDeck: DEXSTR attrition vs sustain/boss enemies. Play early against healers; Septic Bloom / Quarantine keep Infected pressure so the foe is always choosing between rotting and drinking poison.
meta.powerNotes: 3 Infected (~3-9 dmg/turn until wiped) + a combat-long trap at 2 energy R. enchant:other on the foe verified in Bone_Pin.json / Trail_of_Blood.json; ENCH GainHealth trigger verified in Bone_Pin.json (decoded trig 7); enchantment-on-holder afflicts via bless (Blood_Levy.json pattern). theType 'other' + combat mirrors Bone Pin's foe-enchant type (theType 2) made persistent (combatEnchantment 1, Carnage.json). Assumes poison-eaten heals do not fire GainHealth (DoHealing replaces the heal with stack removal) - flagged for sim check to rule out weird loops.

cards.name: Spiteful Ground
cards.description: This combat, deal 1 Piercing damage whenever your foe plays an Action.

meta.whyDifferent: Caltrops is a one-round burst (3+X piercing + 1 Bleeding per foe action, then removes itself). This is the opposite trade: tiny damage, whole combat - and the point is not the 1 damage, it is that Bleeding ticks whenever the holder is DEALT damage (Bleeding.json effectTrigger DealtDamage): every enemy card play becomes a guaranteed bleed tick for the rest of the fight. A permanent tick-frequency engine is a new mode of the mechanic, not Caltrops with different numbers.
meta.intendedDeck: STR bleed-stack decks (Lacerate, Boneskewer, Rupture): keep foe Bleeding high and this drains them on THEIR turn; also pings toward Weeping Blade's condition. Cross-pack: any poison already ticks per enemy play, so both DoTs fire on the same event.
meta.powerNotes: Enemy plays ~3-5 actions/turn: ~4 piercing/turn passive at 2 energy R, plus tick value only in bleed decks (cf. Caltrops U, 1 Neutral, one round). codeLine copied verbatim-shape from Caltrops.json (ENCH trig 39 EnemyPlaysAction, 'directdamage:3+[[enchantmentStacks]]:normal:self') - including its ':self' target token, which Caltrops demonstrably uses to hit the FOE from a self-enchant during the enemy turn. UNVERIFIED: whether directdamage fires DealtDamage and thus actually triggers Bleeding (Caltrops pairing damage with Bleeding suggests yes); needs sim check - if not, the card is still playable but loses the tick-engine role.

cards.name: Metastasis
cards.description:
Double your foe's Afflictions. 
Draw a card for each different Affliction on them ([[otherAfflictions]]).

meta.nearestExisting: Profanity.json (increase foe afflictions by 1/3); Aura_of_Venom.json (doubles Poison only)
meta.whyDifferent: Aura of Venom doubles Poison alone every turn; Profanity adds flat +1/+3 to each. This is a one-shot double of EVERYTHING - Poison, Bleeding, Infected, Decay, Bane - plus draw scaling with breadth ([[otherAfflictions]] counts DISTINCT afflictions, cf. Drain_Soul.json vs otherTotalAfflictions). The reward is explicitly for going WIDE across both archetypes and the Infected angle, which is this pack's whole thesis; mono-poison decks get more from Aura of Venom, so it dodges strictly-better.
meta.intendedDeck: The dual-attrition pile's finisher: Septic Bloom + Lacerate + Ague Fever put 3-4 affliction types out, then Metastasis doubles ~25-35 stacks and refills your hand 3-4 cards.
meta.powerNotes: Legendary 3-cost anchors: Avatar_of_Blight.json (DEX 3 L), Toxic_Bloom.json (DEXSTR 3 L). Value scales with setup investment per spec 4 conditional rule; playCondition (AnyValue IsMoreThan [[otherAfflictions]]:0, idiom verified in Cling_to_Sanity.json play-condition shape) prevents dead draws. doubleafflictions is in effect-commands.txt; shipped usage is only 'doubleafflictions:self' (Falling_Apart.json). UNVERIFIED: the ':other' target argument - if unsupported, fallback is bare 'doubleafflictions' (default-other like most inflict-family commands) or Profanity-style 'increaseafflictions:[[otherAfflictions]]'; needs sim check before shipping. Draw is clamped by hand cap 10 (DeckHandler).

meta.nearestExisting: Warmace.json (only shipped weapon applying an affliction, Brittle=lastDamage at cost 2); Poison_Dart.json (damage:1; inflict:5 Poison at 1 cost)
meta.whyDifferent: The only starting weapon that gives up base damage (1 vs the universal 2) to buy affliction stacks. Poison stacks are duration, not magnitude (Part III) - so a repeating 2-Poison drip means the tick engine never runs dry, which is exactly the axis the pack's payoffs (Potency, tick-frequency) scale on. No shipped weapon makes a stat sacrifice for archetype fuel.
meta.intendedDeck: VenomousLegacy poison shells: Septic Bloom/Ague Fever payoffs stay live without spending deck slots on reapplication.
meta.powerNotes: Budget: 1 immediate + 2 delayed-flat (~1/foe-play) approximates the 2-damage weapon par; strictly worse upfront tempo is the price of the engine. Poison eats incoming foe healing 1:1, a defensive micro-rider already priced into shipped poison commons.

weapons.name: Flaying Hook
weapons.description: Deal [damage:2] damage. Bloodlust: Deal 2 extra damage and apply 1 Bleeding.

meta.nearestExisting: Daggers.json (statline twin: 2 + condition-gated adddamage rider); Cleavers.json (Frenzy-gated bonus + Bleeding)
meta.whyDifferent: Daggers' rider is positional (Ambush - first card of the round); Flaying Hook's is archetype-state (Bloodlust: foe Bleeding or bleed damage dealt this turn). It converts maintained bleed into sustained basic-attack pressure that also re-feeds the bleed - a payoff loop, not an opener. No shipped weapon reads an affliction on the foe.
meta.intendedDeck: STR bleed-tempo (Weeping Blade, Rupture, Spiteful Ground): one bleed source turns every subsequent basic attack into 4 damage + upkeep.
meta.powerNotes: Statline par: base 2 at 1 Neutral. Gated total (4 + 1 Bleeding) needs an external bleed starter and sits below Daggers' shipped ceiling (Ambush = (2+3)*2 = 10, available every round when led). adddamage on trigger None + condition is Daggers' exact encoding.

weaponPowers.name: Festering Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to start next combat with Infected equal to your level ([[myLevel]]) on your foe.

meta.nearestExisting: Baneful Weapon (cd2: startstatus Poison [[myLevel]]*2 on foe + poison-lifegain passive)
meta.whyDifferent: All six shipped Infected users are Monster-rarity - no player card or talent can apply it (pack DESIGN-NOTES flagship gap). This is the run's only faucet: Infected ticks per card the foe discards and only clears when the foe heals, so it pressures discard-heavy enemies in a way Poison/Bleeding cannot.
meta.intendedDeck: VenomousLegacy attrition: Quarantine/Symbiotic Carapace defend while Infected grinds; poison alongside it blanks enemy self-healing (heals are eaten before they clear Infected).
meta.powerNotes: cd2 curve point: level-scaled affliction opener (Baneful = 2xlvl Poison + passive). Runs 1xlvl with no passive because Infected's per-stack value is higher (repeats every foe discard, no natural decay). Command startstatus:X:false is shipped-verbatim; Infected as its referenceStatus is UNVERIFIED (no shipped startstatus user; status asset exists, excludeFromRandom only gates randomstatus rolls) - flagged for in-game QA.

weaponPowers.name: Hemorrhaging Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to inflict Bleeding equal to twice your level ([[myLevel]]*2) at the start of next combat. Passive: Whenever you inflict a Deep Wound, gain 3 HEALTH.

meta.nearestExisting: Serrated Weapon (cd2: startstatus Bleeding [[myLevel]]*2 + Lifedrinker Venom potion); Feral Weapon OLD (cd3: same bleed line alone)
meta.whyDifferent: Serrated's second half is a one-shot potion; Hemorrhaging's is a permanent rider on the Deepwound trigger - the first tier-0 talent that pays off the threshold-execution half of the bleed archetype instead of the stack half.
meta.intendedDeck: Bleed decks running Rupture/deepwound lines; opener guarantees Bloodlust is live for Flaying Hook/Weeping Blade on turn 1.
meta.powerNotes: cd3 curve point: shipped cd3 = 2-3xlvl status or free card. Identical primary payload to Feral OLD (cd3) plus a small conditional rider (heal 3, gated on landing Deep Wounds - max 5/combat via E_maxwounds) mirrors Singing Weapon's on-Kill heal rider scale. UNVERIFIED: Deepwound as a rider trigger has no shipped tier-0 precedent (legal GameTriggers member; heal-on-trigger rider pattern is shipped).
